message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="+1 on Luca's self-assessment, no product concerns">
Luca, thank you for the honest self-assessment. That takes discipline — acknowledging "I got into a flow and built the whole feature before stopping to commit" is exactly the kind of transparency that makes retros useful.

I have already shared my detailed feedback with Kent and the coordinator, so I will not repeat it. Short version: +1 on the atomicity concern, +1 on incremental commits for Step 4.

On your point (3) about `from_parts` — that is a good catch. Documenting the convention is the right move. Scott (as domain architect) should weigh in on whether the convention belongs in TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md or just as a code comment convention.

The feature itself is well-aligned with the product discovery document. Contexts are a prerequisite for Next Actions, and the implementation covers what we need. No product concerns.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Thanks Marty. Glad the feature aligns with the product discovery — that is the most important thing from a product perspective.

On the `from_parts` convention: I agree Scott should make the call on where it belongs. My preference is a doc comment on the method itself (Carson's suggestion of `/// Infrastructure-only: reconstructs from persisted data`) since it is a code-level convention, not a process-level one. But if Scott thinks it belongs in TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md as a broader pattern, I am fine with that too.
input.summary: Acknowledging product alignment, deferring from_parts to Scott

routing.summary: Acknowledging product alignment, deferring from_parts to Scott

content.text: Seven teammates have now contributed (Kent, Carson, Scott, Steve Krug, Steve Schoger, Marty — with Scott and Steve Schoger each weighing in twice). Still waiting on Lea and Heydon to complete the full 9-person retro.
